heading: Compare
subheading: Drop two fonts to preview sample text side by side and see which scripts both support.

fontInput.placeholderFirst: Drop one font here, or choose a file
fontInput.placeholderSecond: Drop another font here, or choose a file

emptyPanel: Drop fonts above to compare them side by side.

share.hint: Optional: uploads both fonts to create a private-by-link comparison. Normal use never uploads; inactive shares are deleted after 10 days.
share.sessionNotFound: This share link is invalid or has expired.
share.sessionError: Could not load the shared comparison.

customText.label: Custom preview text
customText.hint: Used in Text and Waterfall — leave blank to use the default sample.

switches.syncSliders: Synchronize font size and line height

dataTab.empty: Load both fonts to compare their metadata and language support.

characters.captionAll: Showing every character this font maps.

features.empty: Neither font defines any OpenType layout features.
features.heading: Layout features ({count})
features.sharedHint: Both fonts support these — shown with the feature turned on.

languageTable.noDifferences: No language differences between these fonts.

overlay.instructions: Type up to {max} characters to overlay their outlines from both fonts.
overlay.metricGuidesDescription: Dashed lines mark each font's own x-height and cap-height; the solid line is the shared baseline.
overlay.emptyState: Type at least one character above to see its outline overlay.

pairing.heading: Pairing
pairing.empty: Load both fonts to see how their proportions compare.
pairing.disclaimer: This compares geometric proportions only — it can't judge taste. A "Distinct" result may be exactly the contrast you're going for.

about.intro: Compare loads two fonts in your browser and lines them up side by side. Normal comparison stays entirely on your device.
about.sharing: Share links are optional. Fonts are uploaded only when you select Create share link. Anyone with the link can download and view both fonts. Shared sessions are automatically deleted after 10 days without access.
about.body: Text shows reading feel at one size and line height. Waterfall steps through a size ladder so you can spot where a face gets fragile at small sizes or heavy at large ones. Characters shows every glyph each font maps, so you can see coverage gaps at a glance. Features compares which OpenType layout features each font defines — ligatures, small caps, stylistic sets — and previews the ones both fonts share. CSS gives you a ready-to-use stylesheet for each font. Overlay superimposes one glyph from both fonts so you can see shape differences directly. Data compares metadata, metrics, variable axes, and language support, highlighting where the two fonts differ. Pairing compares x-height, weight, and width between the two fonts and explains what the difference will look like — it doesn't tell you if the pairing is good. Type your own text above to use it in Text and Waterfall.
